title: rubus conoacanthus
intro: Rubus conoacanthus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rubus conoacanthus include its ability to support the immune system, reduce the risk of heart disease, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
